name: Axis Select Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Annual fee waived on spending ₹3,00,000 in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 2 EDGE Points per ₹200 spent on all purchases
5X EDGE Points (5 per ₹200) on travel and dining spends

welcomeBenefits.condition: Credited on first transaction within 30 days

additionalBenefits: 5X rewards on travel and dining categories
8 domestic + 4 international lounge visits per year
Low 2% foreign currency markup for international spends
pros: 5X rewards on travel and dining makes it strong for lifestyle spends
8 domestic and 4 international lounge visits is generous for premium tier
Low 2% foreign currency markup saves on international purchases
Annual fee waivable with ₹3 lakh spend threshold
Priority Pass membership included for international lounge access
cons: EDGE Point value at ₹0.40 is lower than competing premium cards
Base earn rate of 2 points per ₹200 is modest for a ₹3,000 fee card
No milestone or annual spend bonus benefits
Welcome benefit of 5,000 points (₹2,000 value) does not fully offset the fee
idealFor: Frequent travellers and diners who want strong category rewards
Users who need both domestic and international lounge access
International spenders looking for low foreign currency markup

faqs.question: How many lounge visits do I get with the Axis Select card?
faqs.answer: You get 8 complimentary domestic lounge visits and 4 international lounge visits per year through Dreamfolks and Priority Pass.

faqs.question: What qualifies as travel and dining for 5X rewards?
faqs.answer: Travel includes airline tickets, hotel bookings, travel agencies, and ride-hailing services. Dining includes restaurants, cafes, food delivery apps, and bars.

faqs.question: How does the 2% foreign currency markup compare?
faqs.answer: The 2% markup is lower than the industry standard of 3.5%. For international transactions, this can save ₹1,500 for every ₹1 lakh spent abroad.

faqs.question: Can the annual fee be waived?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ₹3,000 annual fee is waived if you spend ₹3,00,000 or more in the previous year.
